title: Enable Google account connection for automatic YouTube channel posting

requirements.summary: Modify ChannelConnection component to use real Google OAuth flow instead of mock implementation
acceptanceCriteria: Connect button initiates real Google OAuth flow
Connected state shows the user's Google account email and YouTube channel name
Disconnect button revokes access and clears stored tokens

file_operations.description: Replace the mock OAuth implementation with real Google OAuth 2.0 flow. Initiate OAuth by calling the backend hasGoogleOAuthCredentials() to check connection status, and storeGoogleOAuthCredentials() after receiving the authorization code from Google's OAuth redirect. Update the UI to display the connected Google account email and YouTube channel name from the backend UserProfile. Implement disconnect functionality that clears OAuth credentials by calling backend methods. Remove mock data and extensive debug logging once OAuth flow is working.

Replace mock OAuth implementation with real Google OAuth integration. Use the backend methods hasGoogleOAuthCredentials(), isYouTubeChannelConnected(), connectYouTubeChannel(), and storeGoogleOAuthCredentials() to manage the OAuth flow. Implement proper redirect handling for Google OAuth callback with authorization code exchange. Update connection status checks to use real backend data from UserProfile.youtubeAuth and UserProfile.googleOAuthCredentials. Remove mock OAuth logic and simplify debug logging.

Add queries for checking Google OAuth connection status using hasGoogleOAuthCredentials() and isYouTubeChannelConnected() backend methods. Include these in the existing query invalidation logic when profile updates occur. Ensure the useGetCallerUserProfile hook properly exposes googleOAuthCredentials and youtubeAuth fields from the UserProfile type.

requirements.summary: Update ClipCard to disable YouTube post button when Google account is not connected
acceptanceCriteria: Post to YouTube button is disabled when no Google account is connected
Tooltip or message directs users to connect their Google account first
Button becomes enabled once Google account is successfully connected

file_operations.description: Add connection status check using the useYouTubeChannel hook to determine if a Google account is connected before enabling the YouTube post button. Disable the button when hasGoogleOAuthCredentials() returns false. Display a tooltip or helper text that directs users to the channel connection page to connect their Google account first. Update the button state to become enabled once the OAuth connection is established.

Add pre-flight validation to check if the user has connected their Google account before attempting to post. Use the hasGoogleOAuthCredentials() backend method to verify OAuth tokens exist. Display user-friendly error messages when posting fails due to missing or expired OAuth credentials, directing users to reconnect their Google account. Handle backend authorization errors gracefully with appropriate user feedback.
